req.title: Joint account operations
req.statement: A joint-account holder shall perform transactions; the secondary holder shall not be able to modify ownership.
error_pathways: 403 when secondary holder attempts ownership change

doc.reasoning: R-FIN-018 mandates that joint account holders can perform transactions, but secondary holders must be prevented from modifying ownership. The test suite validates three critical scenarios: (1) primary holder executing a valid transaction (positive, EP), (2) secondary holder attempting ownership modification and receiving 403 (negative, NEG), (3) boundary case testing secondary holder attempting transaction on edge-case account states (boundary, BVA+STC). Tests are deterministic, idempotent, and traceable to the requirement's error pathway and expected outcomes.

tests.name: test_primary_holder_executes_transaction
preconditions: Joint account exists with primary and secondary holders
Primary holder authenticated with valid JWT
actions: Verify response status and transaction record

tests.name: test_secondary_holder_ownership_modification_forbidden
preconditions: Joint account exists with primary and secondary holders
Secondary holder authenticated with valid JWT
trace: R-FIN-018: secondary holder shall not modify ownership ✓

tests.name: test_secondary_holder_zero_amount_transaction_rejected
preconditions: Joint account exists with primary and secondary holders
Secondary holder authenticated with valid JWT
trace: R-FIN-018: operation not applied for invalid input

tests.name: test_secondary_holder_normal_transaction_allowed
preconditions: Joint account exists with primary and secondary holders
Secondary holder authenticated with valid JWT
actions: POST /accounts/{id}/joint with standard transaction payload (no ownership_change)
expected: Secondary holder name in audit trail

tests.name: test_secondary_holder_max_amount_boundary
preconditions: Joint account exists with primary and secondary holders
Secondary holder authenticated with valid JWT
trace: R-FIN-018: operation applied at upper limit
